title: quercus invaginata
intro: Quercus invaginata, also known as the Japanese leather oak, is a species of evergreen tree native to Japan. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Quercus invaginata include reducing fever, alleviating pain, and supporting the immune system, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. Quercus invaginata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for various forms of consumption to harness its medicinal properties.
